reviews.author: TravelbyTin
reviews.text: Wow. Blue river, nice spot for photos. High recommended

reviews.author: PandaPaw P
reviews.text: Spectacular view and chill boatride. The boat stops at a small place on the way back with possibility to buy food and beverages.

reviews.author: J G
reviews.text: I had seen the pass/canyon from above so it was interesting to take a trip up the river We were the only foreigners I saw, it's very popular with Vietnamese people who all take photos at a certain spot You can hire traditional clothing also for your photos Be advised that the road in and out to the boat landing is under construction and extremely rough and slow to negotiate

reviews.author: Trung Nguyen
reviews.text: U have to visit this place when you come to VietNam Nho Quế River, winding through Hà Giang Province in northern Vietnam, is one of the country's most breathtaking natural wonders. Known for its deep turquoise waters, this river cuts through majestic limestone mountains and lush valleys, forming dramatic gorges and steep cliffs, particularly in the iconic Tu Sản Canyon. At an elevation of over 1,500 meters, the river is nestled within the Dong Van Karst Plateau Geopark, a UNESCO-recognized site, showcasing unique rock formations and biodiversity. Many visitors come to marvel at the views from the Ma Pi Leng Pass, which offers panoramic sights of the Nho Quế River winding like a ribbon far below. Boating on the river has also become a popular activity, allowing travelers to navigate its narrow passages, witness the towering cliffs up close, and experience the serene beauty of this mountainous area.

reviews.author: Leona Joyfull Life
reviews.text: Nho Que River with clear green water. The river is deep and winding at the foot of high mountains. The beautiful road always challenges drivers and the unique beauty of the river is the attraction of Nho Que to tourists. (See more Nho Que between Meo Vac and Dong Van districts, is a great place to visit in the Ha Giang loop by motorbike tour. There is a road under construction and it seems that more homestays and hotels are being built. If so, you can stay overnight and enjoy the beauty of Nho Que when the new day begins. (boat tour price: 120,000 VND/pax) #handinhandadventure

reviews.author: Hoàng Trần
reviews.text: If you want to spend more time having fun in Nho Que river area, you should go to Dong Van town to be closer instead of trying to move to Meo Vac town. Evening travel through the roads of Ha Giang is quite dangerous, so accommodation in Dong Van area will be a reasonable choice for your trip.

reviews.author: Edward Robert
reviews.text: Nho Que River is one of Vietnam's stunning rivers, celebrated for its pristine natural beauty and harmonious integration with the surrounding landscape. Flowing through tranquil countryside, rural areas, and lush mountains, this river creates a memorable natural journey.

reviews.author: Cleopatra Eira
reviews.text: My recent visit to Sông Nho Quế, a tranquil river in Vietnam, was a serene escape. The clear waters, lush surroundings, and peaceful atmosphere make it a perfect retreat. Activities like kayaking and swimming in the pristine river add to the experience. The nearby villages offer a taste of local culture and cuisine, while temples and pagodas along the riverbanks provide a glimpse into the area's rich heritage. Sông Nho Quế is a hidden gem for those seeking a peaceful and immersive natural and cultural experience.

reviews.author: Thao Tran
reviews.text: Must-go place in Hà Giang otherwise you will regret! There are two ways going down to boat stops : motorbike or hiking. 1. Motorbike: if you hire xe om, it cost you 150k/person. If you ride your own motorbike, you rather be an excellent rider since it’s dangerous and risky. 2. Hiking: you park your motorbike on the boat house then going down for more than 2km to boat stop at the river. Really tired going up 😂😂😂

reviews.author: BodoiCuHo
reviews.text: Nho Que River in Ha Giang is a breathtaking natural wonder, winding through majestic mountains and deep canyons. The turquoise water, reflecting the lush greenery, creates a stunning landscape. A boat ride offers an unforgettable experience, immersing visitors in the peaceful beauty of Vietnam’s northern highlands.

reviews.author: John Son
reviews.text: The water's so clear and inviting, with misty mornings adding that ethereal touch; it's the kind of place that resets your soul after a hectic travel day. If you're up for it, definitely hop on a kayak or boat ride to glide through hidden bends and spot wildlife along the shores—trust me, the gentle paddling and serene silence will be highlights of your Vietnam trip. I booked my boat tour through the Joytime app, and it was seamless from start to finish; the local guide was knowledgeable, pointing out secret spots, and I felt incredibly lucky to have sunny weather that made the colors pop even more.

reviews.author: Tuan Vu
reviews.text: Undeniably, this proves to be the most charming place to record for video or photo playback… as the year-round solid blueish green tone of the river will be so “out of the world” like and more memorable than any highland type streams… even though the rockky trek by foot from the parking spot to where the boats await is a bit challenging as result from on a wet, rainy day(obliging us to wear mud cover/bags over our shoes… )That is, only when you look back at the videos you took from Nho Que river does it dawn on you on how unique this particular valley has been picture-wise… A not to miss attraction!!

reviews.author: Đình Lê Lê
reviews.text: One of the best view in Ha Giang, must see once in your life

reviews.author: Hồng Nguyễn
reviews.text: very beautiful The address should visit if visiting Vietnam

reviews.author: Nguyên Nhung Lưu
reviews.text: A green and beautiful river with trees and mountains that flows along the road as you travel through Ha Giang. You are likely to see indigenous people doing daily chores on its side. There is a bridge across it, which I happened to be on on a sunny winter day. It was a spectacle.

reviews.author: Trang Cao Thu
reviews.text: Lush green water like yoy have not seen elsewhere

reviews.author: Linh Vu
reviews.text: amazing place, beauty nature for bikers, travellers. Very good for photographers.

reviews.author: Lê Tuân
reviews.text: The Nho Quế river originates from China, flows into Vietnam, forming the Vietnam-China border in the northernmost part of the country in Lung Cu and Ma Le communes, Dong Van district, Ha Giang province, Vietnam. If you want to see the river, you have to buy a ticket for 120,000 VND per person, but you have to wait for about 12 people or more for the boat to leave. Private boat is 1,200,000 VND, the same travel time on the river is about 1 hour to 1.5 hours depending on you. . LE TUAN tour guide. Thanks.

reviews.author: Anh Tung Vu
reviews.text: One of the best kayak places in Vietnam

reviews.author: Zac Crush
reviews.text: Great place to visit off the beaten path. True Vietnam.

reviews.author: Gun Ho
reviews.text: Adventurous ride from highway down to Ta Lung boat harbour. Great views. A 5 star trip.

reviews.author: Hang Nguyen
reviews.text: Speechless! You must come and see yourself

reviews.author: Hòa Vũ
reviews.text: Disappointed with the length of the boat ride. Wish that they go a little further into the river.
